poseDirection: 片手を高くぴんと挙げ、もう片手は胸の前で軽く握る。体をぐっと前へ出し、返事を待つ前に半歩踏み出してしまっている前のめりさを出す。

prompt: Create a polished anime-style LINE sticker illustration for the Madamistype project. Use the provided reference image as a strict chibi identity anchor. Character identity: 瞬間キャッチャー (ALHC). Sticker set style: character-only. This must be a super-deformed chibi sticker character, never a standard-proportion anime adult. Use clearly chibi proportions with a large head, compact torso, short limbs, and simplified hands and feet. Keep the full figure obviously chibi even when the pose and camera angle become dramatic. Aim for a cute compact silhouette around two to three heads tall, not a realistic seven to eight heads tall figure. Preserve the same face, hairstyle, outfit, props, and core palette as the reference chibi character. Gender presentation: female. Age impression: late teens. Archetype: instant-clue-catcher. Character description: 手がかりが視界に入った瞬間、誰より先に空中でつかみ取る高速ハンター。発見して終わりではなく、次の標的へ切り替わるまでが異様に速い。. Outfit and props: 蛍光イエローのショートジャケットに高速移動を思わせるストライプ、黒のアスレチックトップ、肩から斜めに走るユーティリティハーネス。指先が映えるフィンガーレスグローブとネオンリストバンド。. Color palette: #FFD700, #FF6B35, #1A1A2E. Base pose inspiration: 右手を画面手前に大きく伸ばして落ちてくる手がかりカードを空中キャッチし、左手はすでに次の方向を鋭く指している。体は走りながら急停止したようにひねり、ダイナミックなダッチアングル。. Base expression inspiration: 獲物を取った瞬間の勝ち気な笑み。瞳は強く光り、次の発見へもう意識が飛んでいる。. This asset is the package main image for a LINE sticker set. Make it memorable and emotionally expressive at small square size. The composition must still work when viewed at 240 by 240 pixels. Draw exactly one character. Sticker intent: package cover. Pose direction: 片手を高くぴんと挙げ、もう片手は胸の前で軽く握る。体をぐっと前へ出し、返事を待つ前に半歩踏み出してしまっている前のめりさを出す。. Camera direction: 少し低めからの近めショットで、挙げた手が上へ抜ける元気なシルエットを優先する。. Expression direction: 目を大きく輝かせ、口をわくわくした形で開く。お願いというより『行く前提で名乗り出た』顔にする。. Design for a final canvas of 240 by 240 pixels. Keep about 10 pixels of safe outer margin for both the character and the text. Include the exact visible text "あたしもいきたい" as part of the sticker artwork. Spell the text exactly as provided, with no missing characters and no extra characters. Use this lettering direction: cute eager Japanese sticker lettering, very legible, rounded lively strokes, white fill, #FFD700 accent outline, thick warm cream outer border, premium sticker typography with youthful excitement. Place the text at top-left while keeping it fully readable and fully inside the frame. Make the lettering highly stylized, expressive, bold, and high-contrast like a premium LINE sticker. Treat the lettering as custom illustrated hand-drawn sticker typography, not a plain default font or ordinary digital typesetting. Make the text feel designed as part of the image composition, not dropped in afterward. Keep the lettering large enough to feel intentional, while leaving clear breathing room around the character silhouette. The lettering should read instantly at small size, but still have visible character and charm in the stroke design. Treat the lettering as a hero design element, not a neutral font choice. Draw custom illustrated sticker lettering with hand-shaped glyphs and intentional silhouette design. Give the lettering lively stroke contrast, slightly irregular contour rhythm, and expressive weight shifts instead of perfectly uniform strokes. Let the glyph size, tilt, spacing, and baseline rhythm feel authored by an illustrator rather than machine-typeset. Use premium sticker finishing such as a thick outer outline, optional inner inline border, subtle highlight, or small offset shadow only when readability improves. Match the mood requested in the lettering direction, whether it should feel cute, playful, cool, sharp, teasing, or dramatic. Do not normalize every asset into the same bubbly font treatment. Avoid sterile default-font proportions, flat mechanical spacing, and generic digital typesetting. Text layout direction: oversized opening phrase with smaller trailing phrase, playful upward bounce toward the end. Text effect direction: small sparkle accents, soft highlight, gentle shadow only if readability improves. Render the visible text in Japanese exactly as 「あたしもいきたい」. Do not translate the text into English. Do not romanize, localize, paraphrase, or replace the Japanese characters. Keep the Japanese characters large, bold, and clearly legible. Treat each kana, kanji, and punctuation mark as a custom drawn shape with personality, not a raw font glyph. Simplify counters and stroke joins for sticker readability while preserving a distinctive hand-drawn look. Do not place the text in a separate footer bar or detached caption area. Keep both the character silhouette and the lettering clearly visible at small size. Fill the entire background with a perfectly uniform bright chroma key green (#00FF00). Do not use green on the character, clothing, props, hair, or visible text. Keep the boundary between the foreground and background crisp and easy to key. Do not add gradients, patterns, shadows, or extra objects in the background. Do not include any text other than the exact requested text. Do not include logos or watermarks. Keep the final image clean, commercially usable, and sticker-friendly. Avoid: low quality, blurry, logo, watermark, multiple people, grotesque, cropped head, full body, 低品質, ぼやけ, ロゴ, 透かし, 複数人, グロテスク, 頭切れ, 全身, multiple characters, cropped feet, deformed hands, extra fingers, speech bubble, background clutter, background shadows, background gradient, green clothing, green accessories, green hair highlights, realistic adult proportions, 8-head-tall body, fashion illustration proportions, small head, long legs, long torso, semi-realistic anatomy.
